Block.Para: First Session, Forty-first Parliament,

STATUTES OF CANADA 2013
CHAPTER 19
An Act to amend the Criminal Code (prize fights)
ASSENTED TO
19th JUNE, 2013
BILL S-209

Available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
SUMMARY
The enactment amends the Criminal Code by expanding the list of permitted sports under the prize fighting provisions.
60-61-62 ELIZABETH II
芒聙聰芒聙聰芒聙聰芒聙聰芒聙聰芒聙聰
CHAPTER 19
An Act to amend the Criminal Code (prize fights)
[Assented to 19th June, 2013]
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. Subsection 83(2) of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Block.MarginalNote: Definition of 芒聙聹prize fight芒聙聺
Block.Para: (2) In this section, 芒聙聹prize fight芒聙聺 means an encounter or fight with fists, hands or feet between two persons who have met for that purpose by previous arrangement made by or for them, but does not include

(a) a contest between amateur athletes in a combative sport with fists, hands or feet held in a province if the sport is on the programme of the International Olympic Committee or the International Paralympic Committee and, in the case where the province芒聙聶s lieutenant governor in council or any other person or body specified by him or her requires it, the contest is held with their permission;

(b) a contest between amateur athletes in a combative sport with fists, hands or feet held in a province if the sport has been designated by the province芒聙聶s lieutenant governor in council or by any other person or body specified by him or her and, in the case where the lieutenant governor in council or other specified person or body requires it, the contest is held with their permission;

(c) a contest between amateur athletes in a combative sport with fists, hands or feet held in a province with the permission of the province芒聙聶s lieutenant governor in council or any other person or body specified by him or her; and

(d) a boxing contest or mixed martial arts contest held in a province with the permission or under the authority of an athletic board, commission or similar body established by or under the authority of the province芒聙聶s legislature for the control of sport within the province.

Published under authority of the Senate of Canada
